reviews.review_description: It does not promote relaxation and meditation. It has ltopics that are one sided that are Not meditation
Have it so you can walk about App needs more improvement Good app needs improvement Selected movement to make it easier This app can be really beneficial Integrate I've used headspeds for years

reviews.review_title: Control control! You must learn control!
reviews.review_description:
This is a really great app, but I’m not gonna use it because it only has movement by teleport with snap turning.  This cannot be adjusted in any way.  Well, there’s hand tracking, but don’t get me started on how inadequate that is in every app that tries it. The “settings” screen has like 2 buttons on it.  There are apparently a ton of people who get motion sickness from smooth controls.  But what no one seems to recognize, is that some of us get motion sickness from the teleporting.   It is extremely disorienting and disconcerting to abruptly appear and disappear.  I’m not saying get rid of the teleporting, I’m saying provide options for everyone.  It’s darn near standard to have all these options in almost every game.  I can’t imagine it’s much more complicated than implementing a couple APIs that Meta probably provides for free.  there have been a couple of updates since several response posts to reviews like this one, but there are still no movement options.

You didn’t have to make this choice for us and I feel like this is indicative of the problems with this app.  It’s too prescriptive.  You added the explore feature which I appreciate.  But I saw another reviewer complaining that they could only access the first module of each program. The developer response was that the user needed to make sure they had finished every last second of the video before the app would give her credit for finishing it and let her move on.  Whether or not that fixed her problem, the point is that we don’t need the app to do this for us.  We don’t need some algorithm to tell us when we’re ready to move on.  We’re not children and you don’t know how experienced we are with meditation outside the app.  Let US choose the module that WE think we’re ready for.  You can still gamify things with cosmetics or trinkets.  Or at least make the first lower rung of modules available all at once, with the clear incentive of the next rung not playable, but at least visible.   Cast aside this artificial veil.  Make the content in this app freely available to its customers, and then it might seem like it was worth $30.

developer_response.body:
Hi, CapnAvatar! Thank you so much for sharing your thoughtful feedback. We hear your concerns about movement options and understand that teleportation can be just as challenging for some users as smooth controls, and we’ll take this into account as we explore future updates. Feedback like yours directly informs how we shape Headspace XR, and we’ll keep striving to make it a more flexible and supportive space for everyone.

A Mindful Message: If ever you catch yourself chasing after a particular result, just seeing that, noting it, letting it go, and just allowing the body and mind to unfold.

reviews.review_description:
This is the best gift I could have bought myself! if someone really wants to work on themselves to live a peaceful life despite the craziness that's surrounding them every moment of the day, you could take the exercises and activities and bring it into your life. you could just do it privately in your head so no one will even notice! the only thing that people would notice is how much more calm or present you are :) I like that that it give you enough time to relax and get you to a better mindset but at the same time its activities and exercises are not too long to loose your interest.
HERE ARE THE CONS: 
*When I  say that the daily sessions should be 10/15 minutes it shouldn't go overtime in the courses. I am not interested in sitting for a whole 10 minute lecture when I already did 2 activities before. I have a time frame for how long I go on my headset and I dont like when the "today" adds 10 more minutes to my daily. I like to be free before I sign off. when it goes overtime I loose out on my free play and other games/apps that I planed on doing that day! for this reason I only do the 5 minute option because more than that is not accurate.
* I find that some of the exercises and activities are repetitive.
 like why the same kind of breathing exercises just in different backgrounds? 
- also, it would be nice to have more options in the leisure part and not just throwing the flow bursts all the time. maybe there can be a paint pallet in some areas.
- another thing, the hoops. there are a lot of those. maybe some can be substituted with basketball hoops and something else?
I would like to thank the producers and everyone involved in headspace! its great and healthy for us humans!!

developer_response.body:
Hello! Thank you so much for the review. We appreciate the time it took and the thought you put into your feedback. We will definitely bring your suggestions to the team and consider ways in which we can improve the app for a better experience for you!

A Mindful Message:
When we meditate, it's not only to benefit ourselves but also to benefit others.

reviews.review_title: Great Updates Turned This App Around
reviews.review_description:
When I first saw the advertisement for this app, I was super excited and couldn’t wait for its release. However, after trying it, I felt a bit disappointed. I found the experience lonely and disorienting, so I only used it a few times before getting bored and leaving it untouched for several months.

Today, I decided to give it another shot, and to my surprise, the app has improved significantly! I love the new guidance and additional interactive content. It’s great to see that my purchase wasn’t a waste because I now feel motivated to use it again.

To make it even better, I’d suggest incorporating more empowering messages. For example, when throwing the colorful items at the wall, a positive word could appear upon impact. Similarly, other games could display uplifting or motivational messages. The app is already great as it is, but little touches like these could take it to the next level. 😉 I’ll gladly give you that fifth star once I see more empowering messages or words integrated into the games—at that point, this app will be absolutely perfect!

developer_response.body:
Hi Madie!

We are thrilled to hear you gave the app another shot and found it helpful for you! We've learned a lot in our first year on the market and are working hard to improve the experience for you and other users so our app is useful, empowering, and motivating. And thank you for the suggestions, will definitely take those back to the team. Really appreciate your review and kind words, it's responses like these that help us know we're on the right track with our mission of helping people. 

A Mindful Message: Everything changes. Nothing stays the same. When we think about this as an idea or a concept, it can feel overwhelming, but the direct experience of this truth is what ultimately sets us free.

reviews.review_description: The meditation space is really not pleasant, with a bare space and the menu in my peripheral vision when my eyes are open. Also on the course, I can't move past the first session: it doesn't end and move on to session 2, so I have to keep doing session 1. I was really excited to see the app in VR, but it feels unfinished to me. Disappointed.

developer_response.body:
Hey Molliz! We appreciate you taking the time to leave your thoughts. It really helps us as we refine the app. We're sorry you're not loving the meditation space and that the courses are glitching. That is a bug we will have the team look into right away. In the meantime, we recommend checking out some of the other meditations spaces throughout the world which might offer you a more pleasing environment. Box breathing is a personal favorite!

A Mindful Moment:
It's rare that we actually simply witness, simple experience a moment for what it is. I think a sunrise and sunset is a perfect example where there is nothing for us to do in that moment. We don't have to look at the sunset and compare it to a previous sunset. We don't have to wish that it were any different. It simply is as it is in that moment for us to enjoy.

reviews.review_description:
Overall, this app is built really well. I really enjoy the graphics, the pre recorded meditations and the space. 
However, I expected more from a well known app. It feels like they put this together and then just left it there to fend for itself. There are so many really positive things that can be done in here to make it so much more interactive and useful and I really hope they go in that direction. Once you’ve done the pre recorded meditations, and the activities, that’s sort of it. It just needs more. Such great potential here, it would be a shame to let it die after they built the space for it

developer_response.body:
Hi Kristin!

We really appreciate this feedback so thank you for reviewing. We are looking to add and improve the app and hope that it can become a useful tool for you on your mindfulness journey. We're glad you're enjoying it so far and hope we can satisfy your needs to keep you coming back for more in the future. Look out for an update soon!

A Mindful Message: 
Meditation is not a defined path. It's a framework, a way to approach the path you're on. You don't have to live a different way or adopt a new identity. Simply apply the approach the path.

reviews.review_description: Loving my time in Headspace XR! The gorgeous environments and guided exercises make it easy and fun to drop in and practice some mindfulness. I love the added perk that completing exercises gains you new patterns to play around with, as it gives me more incentive to spent time in the app. Truly feel like this is the meditation app I’ve been dreaming of - but, I do hope this is just the beginning of more improvements. Many of the social aspects of the app seem great, but for someone spending time alone in the app many of the areas feel underutilized. I think it’d be great to see more areas added in the future, and to see further exploration of breathing exercises, meditations, and movement activities. It’d be stellar to see more night-themed sleep/wind down exercises and areas as well, and maybe more quests and customization rewards for progress. Overall though, a great app that really helps make mindfulness more engaging and rewarding!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this kind review! We are so pleased to hear that you’re enjoying the app and that it’s making mindfulness engaging for you. This is definitely just the beginning and we hope to continue to update the app with more content and features. Thank you for the suggestions, that is definitely something we will relay back to the team as we think about future updates. More to come!

A mindful message: Meditation is not a thing. This is why it's so difficult to talk about. It's an experience, a changing experience, a changing experience that is unique to each and every person.

reviews.review_description: So i’ve tried a lot of mindfulness apps and devices and they all left me wanting for more - this delivers.  It’s like being in your zone at an interactive exhibit that just pulls you from your daily routine and gives you not only the space but guidance to find your inner peace. Interactive sessions of movement, audio and visuals to ease your mind and help you meditate in ways that make you feel alive. I hope to see more content and success for this app - it would be nice if it could take advantage of v3 hardware to a higher degree but alas, it’s not about being a graphic intensive game but a very well thought out experience. I look forward to my new ritual of finding my inner self and peace  every morning for a few minutes to jump start the day.

developer_response.body:
Sn2006gy, thank you so much for your review! We love to hear that you’re liking Headspace XR! We’re so happy the design and experience resonated with you. Our goal with this app was to give people some guidance and allow them to take a break from the chaos of the real world. We’re working on some performance optimizations so keep an eye out for that coming soon!

A mindful message: We talk about training the mind, but we're not training it to do something. We're rather training it to come to a natural place of rest where we can see life more clearly with a new perspective.

reviews.review_description:
I do like the app a lot but ran into problems with the meditation series. I finished sessions but somehow the system does not capture that, making me repeat the session and not unlocking the next one. 

Also I miss (maybe overlooked it) more unguided meditation possibilities. It would also be nice to have live group sessions with lectures, not necessarily with people one knows already.


Keep up the good work!

developer_response.body:
Hi there! 

Thank you for taking the time to review, we really appreciate you and value your insight. We’re so glad you’re liking the app! We hope to add even more meditation possibilities in a coming content update so we’ll let you know when that becomes available. We decided to limit the group sessions to friends and family that you know so we are able to create a safe space for our users, but we would love to be able to host live group sessions in the future so that’s something we’ll consider. 

As for the technical issues you’re having, it should be fixed in our latest update. You can go update to the latest version now! In the meantime, we hope you continue to enjoy the app and if you haven’t yet, check out the Quick Breath portals throughout the world- they’re a great way to get a quick hit of mindfulness!

A mindful message:
As with life, in meditation we often search for meaning through logic and reason, but meditation is not a theory or a concept that can be grasped. It's an experience of life as it is.

reviews.review_description: Compared to other meditation apps, this app is very unique with its very creative, colorful and futuristic setting.  The firefly and boxy treehouse are my favorite places to breathe and relax.  I also like the multiple meditation portals all over the playground where I can just drop in, reset and ground myself for a few minutes. The meditation teachers are great.  However, the only issue I have with the app is navigating the meditation courses at the Blue Sky Tower.  I started on the "Acceptance" course by Dora.   After I finished session one of the course, I could not go onto session two.  The app keeps bringing me back to session one . Also, like other reviewers mentioned, it would be nice if there are a greater variety of games and activities.  Overall, I am happy with the app. Thank you developers!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the feedback, Yuwen! We really appreciate you taking the time to let us know how your app experience has been. The Boxy Treehouse is one of my personal favorites as well! And we totally agree, the meditation portals you mention (we call them Quick Breaths) are perfect when you’re looking for a shorter meditation. We have noted your issue with the Blue Sky Tower- we hope to push out an update with performance fixes soon. And we are also currently working on new content so hope to add a greater variety of activities for you to continue to enjoy in a future update!

A mindful message: Emotions come and go, but there's something else a little bit deeper, and if we listen—whether it's in our meditation or in everyday life—we can begin to familiarize ourself with that sense of inner peace and calm.

reviews.review_description: I've used the Headspace app (on my phone) for a few years. I really like it and was excited to hear that it was coming to VR. Now that I've had a chance to try the VR version, I have mixed feelings about it. Overall it's been a positive experience, but it's different from what I expected. This isn't a bad thing, it's just different. What I expected was content similar to what I used on the phone app. I was expecting guided meditations, short video clips, music- stories for bedtime,....  So, while I feel that the current content is interesting, definitely a different way to approach meditation and mental health, I would like to see some of the content from the phone app here in the VR version.  I especially like the long term guided meditation classes. As it stands with the current content, there are times when I feel there's just only so much ball throwing and sling shooting I feel like doing. It becomes tiring instead of relaxing. A little more variety would be appreciated.

developer_response.body:
Lisa, we appreciate your feedback and you taking the time to review! It’s so helpful to hear as we’re planning out app updates. 

In this version of Headspace, we really tried to design experiences that deal with different pillars of mindfulness, like play and movement, in addition to the meditation and wind down content you typically find in the mobile app. Scientifically, we know that play is hugely beneficial for our mental health- it’s been shown to reduce stress, increase focus and creativity, improve problem solving and overall well being. And statistically, we are all not playing enough! The beauty of VR is that it gives us the ability to explore all the different versions and extensions that make up mindfulness, including play. But we definitely hear you on wanting more of our traditional content and we are taking this feedback into consideration for future updates! To give you a heads up, when you’ve interacted with the world enough and gained enough flow, you’re able to unlock the Blue Sky Tower where you’ll find a ton of great guided meditations and courses similar to our mobile app content! Think of it like a reward for trying out all of our experiences! And, while you’re working through unlocking the Tower, you’ll find Quick Breaths all over the world, which are mini meditations that can be both relaxing and refreshing while you’re in the world. 

And one last thing, if you have friends with headsets, we encourage you to invite them to share a session with you! The slingshots are even more fun with other people- it’s a completely different experience when shared! You can invite friends by pressing the menu button on your left controller, tapping the menu icon on the map, and by starting a multiplayer session. We hope you give it a try and let us know how it goes!

A mindful message: Creativity is as boundless, spacious and limitless as the sky. We are born with it. It cannot be lost and it cannot be destroyed. It can only be forgotten.

reviews.review_title: A great start but lots to fix
reviews.review_description:
Updated March 20, 2024: I have Roomscale boundary on, However, I just see the world through the camera with some starts. But no special mixed reality meditations. It's quite frustrating.

High level, great stuff but can't give more than 3 stars because the mixed reality seems broken for me. I tried it for the first time but now it no longer works when I go into it. Please fix this.
To start off with, this is a great start and does some very cool things. I'm a new Quest 3 user and haven't bought many games so maybe I'm being too harsh with some of this stuff but I'm a longtime headspace user.
First, the good things. This is a lot of fun to move around in the environment and there's a good variety of meditative actions. It's relaxing and fun when it works.
But there are so many things to fix. 
1) As I said above, the mixed reality just doesn't work after the first time I went in. I really enjoyed it the first time but now can't do it anymore.
2) Navigation is a bit wonky. I find myself trying to pick something up and all of a sudden moving around. Some of it may be user error but it seems very easy to go from throwing an item at a wall to leaving the space.
3) The gamification is a bit odd. Telling me I need to play more right now to get more points feels against the spirit of meditation. Maybe it would be better to get points for daily check ins.
4) Speaking of daily check-ins, it would be good to have Andy's daily headspace meditation available.
5) Hand tracking is especially troublesome. I can't figure out how to either navigate or pick things up effectively. And I can't find instructions on how to do this.
6) I can't find any help documentation or support. I guess this is the best place to put things but I feel there should be something more.
7) It would be great to have a daily routines thing going on so that I could come every day and not have to make decisions about exploring.

developer_response.body:
Hi Rob, thanks for posting and for this thorough feedback! It's so helpful for us to hear directly from users like you (especially mobile app users!) We're so glad to hear that you’ve found some experiences you enjoy in the app! Relaxing and fun are two of the words we used a lot when developing this app so we love to see that you’re finding those to be true. We are currently working on optimizations and enhancements for the app that will be made available in an update in the near future. 

For the MR issues you’re having, we would suggest starting over with your room set up. Both Mixed Reality experiences require the Room Set Up to be enabled in the Quest, and we found that clearing the room data can sometimes help clear the issue. To clear existing Room data, go into Settings > Physical Space > Space set up and under Manage spaces delete the Room you’re in and you’ll be able to reset the room again. We hope this fixes the issue for you! 

Thanks for your notes on navigation and hand tracking. We are working on enhancements to navigation and hand tracking and hope to share that in an update soon. We want our users to enjoy navigating throughout the world so if you find that you’re not finding that method of movement working for you, we recommend using the map and jumping into the individual activities directly. To access the map, tap the menu button on your left controller, and tap on each activity on the map to open to open the “Jump in” button and you’ll find yourself transported into that experience. Let us know if that’s a helpful alternative for you!

We appreciate your thoughtfulness around gamification and daily routines and meditations. We want to hear what users would prefer or like as additions so we will be taking your thoughts into account as we develop content updates. Our current reward system is meant to be a gentle encouragement to continually come back to the app and interact with the world, therefore creating a mindfulness habit. It’s good to know other recommendations on what might encourage users to return and stay consistent with their practice. We hope you continue to enjoy the different playful and relaxing experiences within the app- we know mindfulness is a practice and consistency is key!

A mindful message: Meditation can feel unnatural at first. This has nothing to do with the experience of meditation but rather the comparison of that experience to the rest of our existence. So, for most of us, we're living such a busy life now. So, this experience of stepping out of all of that and simply being present can feel like a really alien experience. But the beauty of it is that if we train in it consistently, then we can start to bring that quality of being into other areas of our life, so that all the busyness, the doing, it doesn't feel so stressful, it doesn't feel so hectic. Instead, it has a quality of ease about it.

reviews.review_description: I have never felt this relaxed in my life, I would definitely appreciate if there can be a public social hub in this game instead of it being friend inviting only. That’s all, highly recommend. Update: the silence your voice or any mixed reality mode will not work. I’m not sure if it’s because I’m playing on quest but it will not start.

developer_response.body:
Gabechil- thank you for your comments! We made the app invite only for multiplayer as a safety precaution to create a safe space for you and your friends or family to play and practice being mindful together. We hear your feedback on making it public and will take it into consideration for future updates. For mixed reality, we are currently working on some performance optimizations that will hopefully resolve your issue soon.

If you find that you like the relaxing content within the app, we would suggest trying out the Firefly Treehouse- it’s a great way to unwind and increase your mind-body connection at the same time, perfect for right before bedtime.

A mindful message: When we pause, we reconnect to ourselves, to others, to nature, to the world around us. Even without saying anything, even without doing anything, we reconnect in this way.

reviews.review_title: Lots of potential, but own goals to fix!
reviews.review_description:
Brief first impressions,

Wonderful art style and genuinely great options for mindfulness and meditation. 

The sound and Spatial Audio in particular is VERY good and provides much of the feeling you might get from ASMR in terms of relaxation and chills.

However, lacking any sort of accessibility and controls feels like basic XR stuff now. No smooth locomotion, no smooth turning? No ability to turn off vignettes during the teleport locomotion?

Beyond that optimisation for Quest 3 is lacking. It shimmers like a Q2 game and the frame rate is all over the place. Any actions, even with UI I encountered stutters, and the General frame rate at times feels like 36fps using ASW to achieve 72fps.

This has so much potential, please developers, tell me you’re working on fixing or at least improving these issues. Currently they provide the antithesis to your goals, it feels restrictive and the frame is uncomfortable bordering nauseating. Easy 4 star otherwise.

developer_response.body:
Update: Hey there! We just wanted to let you know your issue should have been fixed in our most recent update. We’d love it if you would jump back in and tell us about your experience! 


Thank you for the review, DeeSax! We appreciate the feedback and are taking it into account as we work to enhance the app. We’re actively working on performance optimizations which should help with some of the issues you’re seeing. We'd opted against smooth locomotion as this was triggering motion sickness for some people, so instead we're now looking to add this as an option in the settings menu in a near update.

We’re glad to see you’re enjoying the content inside the app! If you haven’t already, check out the Boxy Treehouse, it’s one of our personal favorites and is great for winding down.

A mindful message: Rather than thinking of meditation as yet another to-do, consider the possibility that, in actual fact, this is the one time in the day when you don't have anything to do at all.

reviews.review_description:
When I read about this app, I went and got it immediately. I’ve been waiting for a good meditation/mindfulness VR experience. $30 is steep, but I was willing to try.

Unfortunately, this isn’t it. For baffling reasons - they’ve ignored some fundamental VR rules, making this a headache-inducing mess. I’m not able to move around smoothly or adjust the rotation angle, leaving me to wildly pop around the world disoriented.

It’s also not optimized for Quest 3, so the frame rate stutters constantly and I can barely read the text due to resolution and aliasing.

That said, there’s a lot of potential here. With just a few tweaks this could go from 1 to 5 stars. I hope they fix it. Until then, I’m refunding.

developer_response.body:
Update: Hey there! We just wanted to let you know your issue should have been fixed in our most recent update. We’d love it if you would jump back in and tell us about your experience! 

Albert- We’re sorry to hear that movement through the app was difficult and uncomfortable for you. We do suggest teleporting throughout the world by using the map and jumping in to the various activities as an alternative to the locomotion and seeing if that method works better for you. We'd opted against smooth locomotion as this might trigger motion sickness to some users, but it's good to hear that there's an appetite for it so we're looking to add this as an option in the settings menu soon.

We are actively working on performance enhancements as we speak and hope to push out those updates soon. We really appreciate you taking the time to provide your feedback- it’s what will help us make this app the best it can be.

A mindful message: We might think that we're sitting down with the same mind each time we meditate, but like sitting down at the edge of a flowing stream, we're never looking at the same water twice.

reviews.review_description: But please, add subtitles.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review and rating Valerio! Our goal is to make the app as accessible for as many people as possible so we appreciate your suggestion. We plan to add subtitles as an update soon. We hope you continue to enjoy and explore Headspace XR. If you haven’t already, I would suggest checking out the Boxy Treehouse, it’s so relaxing and one of my favorite meditations!

A mindful message: Sometimes we might feel guilty about taking the time out for ourselves to meditate, but if we don’t look after our own mind, then we’ll never be able to help others, and we’ll never be able to reach our full potential.

reviews.review_title: A Peaceful Escape During Troubled Time
reviews.review_description:
I’ve recently been going through a very challenging period in my life, and I can honestly say that the Headspace app has been a wonderful source of comfort and peace. Initially, I wasn’t sure how much I would get out of it, but after a couple of days, I found myself loving it.

One of the most significant benefits of this app is how it helps me examine my emotions. It has a gentle way of guiding you through introspection, which has been incredibly valuable to me. The overall atmosphere is so peaceful and calming—it’s a perfect escape when life feels overwhelming.

I also had the chance to try out the multiplayer mode with my granddaughter, and it was a delightful experience. Setting it up was super easy, and she was able to join my game without any hassle. We spent time painting walls together, and it added an extra layer of joy to the experience.

If you’re looking for a game that offers both emotional support and a serene environment, I highly recommend giving Headspace a try. It’s been a bright spot in my day, and I can’t imagine not having it as part of my routine now.

developer_response.body:
Hi there! We are so pleased to hear that you're finding the app beneficial! We hope that you can continue to find it helpful for dealing with all of your emotions as you navigate life's ups and downs. 

And we love to hear that you've tried it with your granddaughter! That's exactly why we created that feature- to allow for more connection in our lives even when we're not physically together. We hope you both continue to enjoy together. 

A Mindful Message: If we're able to step back, if we're able to witness the thoughts, the feelings as they arise, as they pass away, we start to see that, actually, everything is transient. We don't need to get involved. We don't need to try and control the thinking. Instead, when we step back and observe.

reviews.review_title: I really enjoy this VR app
reviews.review_description: I was curious about this so I tried it. I love it! I never used the mobile app. I love the virtual environment, and the calming and fun activities to do.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the feedback, Glenda! We’re so happy to hear that you’re enjoying your experience! We designed the app so you get a mix of energizing, playful, and relaxing activities to do so that no matter what mood you’re in or what you’re dealing with that day- we can be there for you. If you haven’t tried it yet, I would recommend the mixed reality experience, Quiet Your Inner Voice. It’s a really beautiful and soothing experience. Enjoy!

A mindful message: Rather than thinking of meditation as yet another to-do, consider the possibility that, in actual fact, this is the one time in the day when you don't have anything to do at all.

reviews.review_description: This might legitimately be the best experience I’ve ever had in VR. I’ve never felt so calm and collected before, and the fact that these feelings are always present, and easy to achieve.. it’s just so hard to believe to me. The app itself is so well made. The environments are such a pleasant escape from reality, and all the exercises are really well thought out. I really can’t wait to see how this app expands in the future. 🧡

developer_response.body:
Hi Sneethan! Thank you for this review, we are so touched that you’re finding this app helpful for you! Our most important goal with this app is to give users an experience that truly can benefit them, in and out of headset. We hope to continue to expand the world and bring you new experiences!

A mindful message: Watching the breath is not something we need to reserve for our meditation alone. It's with us wherever we go, an anchor for our focus, a place to rest our mind, ever present until it's not.
